0.q: RNA-seq data showed that the expression levels of TAA1/TAR and YUCCA families, as well as CYP79B2/3, were differentially regulated in NR/CR, PR/CR and PR/NR, suggesting that BoPep4 application may affect the biosynthesis of IAA under NaCl treatment.

2.q: BolMYB28‐BolMAM1 confers tolerance to salt and drought stresses mainly by accelerating the biosynthesis of certain specific aliphatic GSLs.

3.q: ABA homoeostasis was modulated to accumulate more ABA in OEX‐BolMAM1 transgenic broccoli.

4.q: RD2, RD22, VOZ2, LSM1B and MDH physically interacted with bolTLP1, which implied that bolTLP1 could directly interact with these proteins to confer abiotic stress tolerance in broccoli.

5.q: RD2, RD22, VOZ2, LSM1B and MDH physically interacted with bolTLP1, which implied that bolTLP1 could directly interact with these proteins to confer abiotic stress tolerance in broccoli.

6.q: RD2, RD22, VOZ2, LSM1B and MDH physically interacted with bolTLP1, which implied that bolTLP1 could directly interact with these proteins to confer abiotic stress tolerance in broccoli.

7.q: RD2, RD22, VOZ2, LSM1B and MDH physically interacted with bolTLP1, which implied that bolTLP1 could directly interact with these proteins to confer abiotic stress tolerance in broccoli.

8.q: RD2, RD22, VOZ2, LSM1B and MDH physically interacted with bolTLP1, which implied that bolTLP1 could directly interact with these proteins to confer abiotic stress tolerance in broccoli.

9.q: BolMAM1, a key gene in the aliphatic GSL biosynthesis pathway, was identified in broccoli.

10.q: BolMYB28 was demonstrated to directly bind to the promoter of BolMAM1 and activate its transcription.
